name: Sambuca Biscotti
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Unsalted butter; cold, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Place anise seeds in a food processor and pulse until finely ground.
2. Add flour, baking powder, lemon zest, and salt to the food processor with the anise seeds. Pulse until combined.
3. Add butter pieces and pulse until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
4. Add 1 cup toasted pine nuts and sugar; pulse until all ingredients are evenly distributed.
5. In a bowl, whisk eggs and sambuca liqueur together.
6. Add the egg mixture to the flour mixture and pulse briefly to combine.
7. Shape the dough into two equal logs on a lightly floured surface. Place the logs 4 inches apart on an ungreased baking sheet.
8. Bake at 325°F for 35 minutes, or until firm. Loosen edges with a metal spatula; transfer to a cutting board.
9. Reduce oven temperature to 300°F.
10. Cut each log into 1/2-inch thick slices, arranging them cut side down on the baking sheet.
11. Bake for an additional 15 minutes, turning the slices after 7 minutes for even drying.
12. Allow biscotti to cool completely before serving.
This recipe yields approximately 42 biscuits. Each biscuit contains 88 calories, 1 gram of protein, 10 grams of carbohydrates, 5 grams of fat, and 15 milligrams of cholesterol and sodium.
